testFiles.purpose: Track manifest and random selection logic

tests.name: TRACKS array is non-empty
tests.description: The manifest must contain at least one track for the player to function

tests.name: all TRACKS entries start with /audio/
tests.description: Tracks are served from the public/audio directory so all URLs must use that prefix
assertions: every entry in TRACKS starts with '/audio/'

tests.name: pickTrack returns a string from the TRACKS array
tests.description: Without a previous argument, pickTrack should return one of the known tracks
assertions: TRACKS includes the return value of pickTrack()

tests.name: pickTrack avoids immediate repeat when multiple tracks exist
tests.description: When given a previous track URL and more than one track exists, the returned track must differ

tests.name: pickTrack returns the only track when one track exists
tests.description: Edge case: with a single-entry manifest, the function must still return that track even if it matches previous

tests.name: does not access localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies
tests.description: Privacy constraint: track selection must not use any persistent storage

testFiles.purpose: Audio analysis graph setup and data accessors
testFiles.setupNotes: Requires AudioContext, AnalyserNode, and GainNode stubs since jsdom does not implement Web Audio API. Create minimal mock classes in beforeEach that track connections and expose necessary properties (fftSize, frequencyBinCount, connect, getByteFrequencyData, getByteTimeDomainData).

tests.name: createAnalyser returns analyser, gainNode, and connectSource
tests.description: The factory must return all three pieces needed by the player

tests.name: connectSource wires source through analyser to gain to destination
tests.description: The audio graph must route source → AnalyserNode → GainNode → destination so analysis continues while muted

tests.name: analyser has expected fftSize
tests.description: The fftSize should be 1024 or 2048 for meaningful frequency resolution

tests.name: getFrequencyData returns Uint8Array of correct length
tests.description: The returned array length should match analyser.frequencyBinCount

tests.name: getTimeDomainData returns Uint8Array of correct length
tests.description: The returned array length should match analyser.frequencyBinCount

tests.name: does not access localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies
tests.description: Privacy constraint

testFiles.purpose: Muted autoplay with graceful fallback, state management, and user-initiated unmute
testFiles.setupNotes: Requires comprehensive Web Audio API mocks: AudioContext (with state, resume, close, createMediaElementSource, createGain, destination), GainNode (gain.value, connect), AnalyserNode (connect, fftSize, frequencyBinCount), HTMLAudioElement (play, pause, src, addEventListener). Use vi.resetModules() in beforeEach for fresh imports. Mock play() as a resolved or rejected promise to simulate autoplay success/failure.

tests.name: initAudio resolves without throwing when play() succeeds
tests.description: Happy path: autoplay is allowed, play() resolves, player is returned

tests.name: initAudio resolves without throwing when play() rejects (autoplay blocked)
tests.description: Critical AC: autoplay failure must not crash the application
tests.acceptanceCriteria: Autoplay failure is handled without crashing

tests.name: initial state is muted
tests.description: Critical AC: playback must begin in muted mode
tests.acceptanceCriteria: Playback is attempted in muted mode

tests.name: gain is set to 0 before play() is called
tests.description: Muted autoplay means gain must be zeroed before the play attempt, not after
tests.acceptanceCriteria: Playback is attempted in muted mode
assertions: gainNode.gain.value === 0 at the time play() is invoked

tests.name: state is 'playing' when autoplay succeeds
tests.description: When play() resolves, the player should report it is actively playing

tests.name: state is 'suspended' when autoplay is blocked
tests.description: When play() rejects, the player should report suspended state for later resumption

tests.name: setMuted(false) sets gain to 1 and calls ctx.resume()
tests.description: Critical AC: user action must be able to enable audio
tests.acceptanceCriteria: Audio can later be enabled by user action

tests.name: setMuted(true) sets gain to 0
tests.description: Re-muting should zero the gain without tearing down the audio graph

tests.name: setMuted(false) updates muted property to false
tests.description: The muted flag must reflect the current mute state

tests.name: getAnalyserNode returns an AnalyserNode
tests.description: The render loop needs access to the analyser for frequency data
assertions: player.getAnalyserNode() is defined and is the AnalyserNode instance

tests.name: destroy() closes AudioContext and pauses audio
tests.description: Cleanup must release browser audio resources

tests.name: does not use localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies
tests.description: Privacy constraint: audio player must not persist any state

tests.name: audio graph allows analysis while muted
tests.description: Critical AC: analyser must sit before gain in the chain so data flows even at gain=0
tests.acceptanceCriteria: Visuals continue even if autoplay is blocked
assertions: AnalyserNode is connected in the graph
getAnalyserNode() returns non-null even when muted

AudioContext.notes: state should be configurable ('running' | 'suspended') to test both autoplay outcomes. resume() returns a resolved promise.

AnalyserNode.notes: frequencyBinCount = fftSize / 2. getByteFrequencyData/getByteTimeDomainData fill the passed Uint8Array.

GainNode.notes: gain should be an object with a value property that can be read and written.

HTMLAudioElement.notes: play() returns a promise. Mock it to resolve for autoplay-allowed tests and reject with a NotAllowedError for autoplay-blocked tests.

Playback is attempted in muted mode: tests/audio/player.test.ts: gain is set to 0 before play() is called
Autoplay failure is handled without cras: tests/audio/player.test.ts: initAudio resolves without throwing when play() rejects (autoplay blocked)
tests/audio/player.test.ts: state is 'suspended' when autoplay is blocked
Visuals continue even if autoplay is blo: tests/audio/player.test.ts: audio graph allows analysis while muted
Audio can later be enabled by user actio: tests/audio/player.test.ts: setMuted(false) sets gain to 1 and calls ctx.resume()
tests/audio/player.test.ts: setMuted(false) updates muted property to false

conventions.imports: import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
conventions.moduleIsolation: vi.resetModules() in beforeEach with dynamic imports for modules that create singletons
conventions.privacyChecks: Every test file includes a privacy test verifying no localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookie access
conventions.mockPattern: vi.fn() for functions, vi.spyOn() for existing APIs, Object.defineProperty for read-only properties
conventions.restoreMocks: Handled globally by vitest.config.ts restoreMocks: true
